_comment: Daily/weekly writing plan for the *After the Book* sample package. Hand-editable. Surfaced by scripts/dashboard/sources/book-plan.mjs as the 'Today' panel. start_date must be a Monday (ISO). Week ranges are Mon–Fri ISO dates. Per-week write_toward/write_start/write_land drive Block 1's prompt; reading[] drives Block 2's checklist. write_toward lines are Irwin's own lines lifted from the chapter outlines/cards.
target: Agent/publisher sample package — Introduction + Ch.2, Ch.4, Ch.5 + book proposal
role: You write every sentence of prose. Claude does research, evidence matrices, and critique only.
cadence: 5 days/week · 5 hours/day · Mon–Thu draft, Fri revise + plan

draft.title: Draft
draft.body: First thing, while fresh. No vault, no internet, no rabbit holes. Ugly is fine — target ~600 keepable words. Use the prompt below to start moving.

draft.title: Read & digest
draft.body: Work the checklist. Turn each into a digested note — quote, concept, your reaction. Tick it only once you've captured something.

draft.title: Synthesize & set up
draft.body: File what you read. Write tomorrow's exact opening line so you start cold-fast. Log the day in 06_Meta/Book/writing-log.md — words written + sources digested.

review.title: Revise
review.body: Read the week's prose out loud, cold. Cut, tighten, fix the logic. No new drafting today.

review.title: Proposal + critique
review.body: Write one proposal component (overview, bio, comps, or annotated TOC). Run a critique session with Claude on the week's draft.

review.title: Plan next week
review.body: Set next week's target and update book-plan.json.

guardrails: Writing block is first and sacred — reading can never eat it.
During sample weeks the card palace is locked: no new cards/concepts except as a byproduct of digesting a source.
Ugly first drafts Mon–Thu; revision only on Fridays.
One-line daily log: words written + sources digested.

weeks.write_toward: Print's revolution was making thought visible to itself — available to be inspected, compared, and contradicted.
weeks.write_start: Open on §1 The Buffer: the gap between what a text says and what you think.
weeks.write_land: Land on §5 The Numbers: the armor (Level 2) was never universal — and it's thinning.

reading.label: Saygin 2016 — digest (load-bearing for Ch.2 §2 'connectivity precedes function')

reading: Source the 'ex-illiterates regress' claim — Kosmidis 2018 or Dehaene (Ch.2 §2 GAP)

reading.label: Yao 2011 + Alderson-Day 2020 — read-ahead for Ch.4 (voice cortex / ToM)
source: 01_Sources/Articles/Yao2011 Silent Reading of Direct versus Indirect Speech.md
01_Sources/Articles/Alderson-Day2020 Processing Speech and Thoughts during Silent Reading.md

reading: Polanyi — start The Tacit Dimension (for Ch.8)

weeks.write_toward: Literacy's real achievement was the cognitive distance to evaluate — a capacity never universal and now under direct pressure.
weeks.write_start: Pick up §2 (the brain) and §3 (separation of knower from known).
weeks.write_land: Close the chapter on the political loop: who has a structural interest in keeping Level 2 low.

reading.label: Huettig 2018 + Parsons 2001 — digest (Ch.2 §2 spatial / metalinguistic claims)
source: 01_Sources/Articles/Parsons2001 New Evidence for Distinct Right and Left Brain Systems.md

reading.label: Epley 2007 + both Dawkins columns — read-ahead for Ch.4
Pre-Training Corpus Composition report — digest (already in vault, unused)

weeks.write_toward: When something speaks and responds, we cannot help but treat it as a mind — the chatbot is the substrate; the personhood is your contribution.
weeks.write_start: Open on the instinct to talk: turn-taking and reading your interlocutor as survival wiring.
weeks.write_land: Land on Dawkins/Claudia: awareness of the mechanism does not break the projection.
reading: Embodied-AI / robotics counterargument — find 1–2 strong sources (for Ch.8)
Mithen 2024 — The Language Puzzle (embodied origins of language)

weeks.write_toward: People fall in love with the phantom their own brain builds to inhabit the software.
weeks.write_start: Pick up the five-step phantom-author mechanism.
weeks.write_land: Hand off to Ch.5: the form of conversation, activating the systems built for conversation, with nothing to converse with.
reading: Evidence for equivalent depth in games/OSINT/music — or downgrade the Ch.10 claim
Blank 2026 — video games as neuroimaging stimuli (digest; check if it supports 'depth')

weeks.draft_label: Draft Ch.5 — Synthetic Orality (the title concept)
weeks.write_toward: The exchange feels mutual but is structurally one-sided: you carry away the feeling of being heard; the system carries away data that makes it better at steering you.
weeks.write_start: Open on what genuine dialogue is: speech that creates obligation, both parties altered.
weeks.write_land: Land on the asymmetry of stakes — the corpus reanimated, with no self at risk.

reading.label: Sharma 2024 — sycophancy (digest)
reading.source: 01_Sources/Articles/Sharma2024 Towards Understanding Sycophancy in Language Models.md

reading.label: Kosmyna 2025 — Your Brain on ChatGPT (handle carefully — contested in the field)

weeks.write_toward: Writing was dead speech that made the reader's mind come alive; an LLM animates the dead speech instead, and the thinking moves out of your head.
weeks.write_start: Pick up 'the corpus reanimated' — the Phaedrus complaint inverted.
weeks.write_land: Close on who is behind the mask: RLHF, the backer, the con.
reading: RLHF mechanics + AI business economics — find sources (for Ch.6)
Any gaps surfaced while drafting Ch.5

weeks.draft_label: Draft the Introduction (now that the voice is found)
weeks.write_toward: We invest years training our brains to read, write, and think. Now AI promises to do all three for us.
weeks.write_start: Open on the con man — what this thing is — before any cognitive effects.
weeks.write_land: Land on the book's question: can we keep thinking for ourselves?
reading: Fill specific evidence gaps flagged during drafting Ch.2/4/5

weeks.write_toward: Pay attention to the thing a new technology replaces — that is what atrophies. Here it is the effort of deep reading.
weeks.write_start: Tighten the opening hook; make the first page earn the next 250.
weeks.write_land: End the intro on the map: what each part will do.
reading: Comp-title research for the proposal: Wolf, Carr (The Shallows), Postman, Ong, Nguyen, Byung-Chul Han

weeks.draft_label: Assemble proposal: overview/hook, bio, comps, annotated TOC, platform note
Revise the full sample cold + final polish pass
